dialogueChunks.intro: I stood my ground, {player_name}. When the {enemy_type} came, I didn't run. Not this time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} fought beside me at the barricade. Didn't say a word, but when it was over, they handed me their waterskin and sat next to me. We watched the sun come up in silence. That silence said more than any apology. {quest_focus} is safe, and {quest_threat} finally eased. For the first time in years, I feel like I belong.
dialogueChunks.outro: You gave me the push I needed to stop running from {quest_origin}. Take this: it's everything I have.

playerResponses.text: Standing your ground when it mattered: that's who you are now.
Silence and a shared waterskin. Sometimes that's enough.

dialogueChunks.intro: The medicine reached {location}, {player_name}. Every vial, properly dosed, properly labeled.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} figured out who sent it. They sent a message: 'The sick are recovering. Come home if you want to.' I read those words fifty times. {quest_stakes} won't repeat. For the first time, the door isn't closed. {quest_focus} is being undone, one remedy at a time.
dialogueChunks.outro: You carried my penance across dangerous ground. This is for you, and I hope you know what it means.

playerResponses.text: Go home. The door's open. That's what matters.
You can't change the past. But you changed the future for those people.
Don't waste the chance. Chances like this don't come twice.

dialogueChunks.intro: The memorial is finished, {player_name}. It's... it's beautiful.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I went at dawn. The {quest_item} catches the light just right, and wildflowers are growing around the base. {target_npc} was there. He'd left a small wooden toy at the foot: his daughter's favorite. He looked at me and said, 'She would have liked this.' Then he walked away. Not in anger. Just in grief. {quest_focus} is honored at last.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't have peace yet. But {quest_threat} is lighter now. You made that possible. Take this, please.

playerResponses.text: The beginning of peace is more than most people find. Hold onto it.
She would have liked it. That's all the redemption you need.
